new_classes.label_en: Blended Scotch Whisky in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22083079)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Scotch Whisky in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22083079)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch whisky – a spirit distilled from a mash of cereals, distilled and matured in Scotland, consisting of a blend of malt and grain Scotch whiskies, marketed at an alcoholic strength by volume of 40 % vol or higher, presented in containers of more than 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2208 30 79 (Chapter 22). Excludes whiskies with added aerated water (whisky-soda).

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the main distinguishing criteria for CN 22083079: (1) it is Blended Scotch Whisky (subclass of BlendedScotchWhisky), (2) in containers of more than 2 litres (maxContainerVolumeL > 2), (3) alcoholic strength ≥ 40 % vol, (4) distilled and matured in Scotland (ScotlandDistillationOrigin), (5) from multiple distilleries / blended (MultipleDistilleryOrigin), (6) excludes whisky-soda (complement of SodaWaterAddition), and (7) is disjoint from the small-container sibling (CN 22083071). A minor gap remains: the legal text notes that the product is 'distilled from a mash of cereals', which is a general whisky requirement already captured at the parent BlendedScotchWhisky/Spirit level and not re-stated here explicitly as a property restriction. The blending of malt and grain whiskies (defining 'blended' at the legal level) is captured via MultipleDistilleryOrigin and the BlendedScotchWhisky parent, but a dedicated MixedGrainMashBasis restriction is not added to avoid over-constraining the blended category (which may use purely malt or purely grain sub-components).
